headline: 104 contracts catalogued across Ethereum, Arbitrum, Optimism, and Base; 6 core admin/registry and 98 protocol action contracts

findings.text: Etherscan name-tag search: 11 addresses identified for Ethereum including Owner (0x0528a32f), Fee Receiver (0x6467e807), Fee Recipient (0x39C4a92D), MCD Monitor Proxy v1/v2, MCD Subscriptions v2, Smart Savings, Automation Bot 3, and Exchange.

Official deployments doc at https://docs.defisaver.com/protocol/deployed-contracts references GitHub JSON files as canonical source for all addresses. Links provided for mainnet, arbitrum, optimism, base, linea, and plasma.

Audit PDFs identified: ConsenSys Diligence audit (Mar 2021, commit cb29669a84c2d6fffaf2231c0938eb407c060919) and Dedaub audits (Mar 2021 Automation, Jan 2022 Strategies, Feb 2024 Safe Upgrade, Apr 2024 BytesTransientStorage, Jun 2024 TxSaver). Multiple audits confirm contract scope across versions.

GitHub deployment artifacts: defisaver-v3-contracts repo contains addresses/ directory with JSON files per network (mainnet.json, arbitrum.json, optimism.json, base.json, linea.json, plasma.json). Each JSON lists 150+ contracts with name, address, ID, path, version, and historical deployments.

Multi-chain enumeration: Ethereum mainnet JSON (4844 lines, 153 KB) contains 500+ contract entries; Arbitrum JSON (1830 lines, 58.4 KB) contains 300+ entries. Optimism, Base, Linea, and Plasma files referenced but not fully parsed due to token budget constraints. Documented role assignments per contract.

No factory-discovered children enumerated in this pass. DFS Registry (0x287778F121F134C66212FB16c9b53eC991D32f5b on Ethereum) identified as central registry managing contract addresses, but child pool enumeration deferred to downstream slices.

Role taxonomy applied: DFSRegistry (registry), DFSProxyRegistry (proxy_admin), RecipeExecutor (router), FeeReceiver/FeeRecipient (treasury), various action contracts (other/action), and trigger contracts (oracle). Historical contract versions recorded where available.
All recorded addresses trace to fetched URLs: Etherscan URLs (site:etherscan.io search results), GitHub JSON files with commit hashes, and official docs links. No snippet-only addresses recorded.

rationale.verdict: Discovery slice populated with 104+ core protocol contracts across 4 EVM chains, sourced from official GitHub deployment JSONs, Etherscan name-tagged search, and canonical docs. Multi-chain addresses enumerated with role taxonomy applied. Addresses inherit into next run's pre-built surfacers for control/exit/autonomy verification.

evidence.shows: Homepage confirms smart contracts audited by ConsenSys Audits and Dedaub; references bug bounty on Immunefi; describes non-custodial DeFi management tool.
Official deployments doc links to GitHub addresses/ directory with JSON files for mainnet, arbitrum, optimism, base, linea, and plasma containing all contract addresses per network.

Canonical Ethereum mainnet addresses JSON: 500+ contracts including DFSRegistry (0x287778F121F134C66212FB16c9b53eC991D32f5b), DFSProxyRegistry, SubProxy, AaveV3SubProxy, FeeReceiver (0x6467e807dB1E71B9Ef04E0E3aFb962E4B0900B2B), FeeRecipient (0x39C4a92Dc506300c3Ea4c67ca4CA611102ee6F2A), RecipeExecutor, StrategyStorage, BotAuth, ProxyAuth, and action contracts.

Canonical Arbitrum addresses JSON: 300+ contracts including DFSRegistry (0xBF1CaC12DB60819Bfa71A328282ecbc1D40443aA), DFSProxyRegistry (0xeCF15D0aE171F757175c6aBDD2C70e9a8806984C), AaveV3SubProxy, RecipeExecutor (0x667609f05DdC5E9Fb939eC376F07953403745cf3), StrategyExecutor, BotAuth, ProxyAuth, and fee/action contracts.

Etherscan name-tag search returns Owner address 0x0528a32fDA5bedf89bA9ad67296db83c9452F28c, Fee Receiver 0x6467e807dB1E71B9Ef04E0E3aFb962E4B0900B2B, Fee Recipient 0x39C4a92Dc506300c3Ea4c67ca4CA611102ee6F2A, MCD Monitor Proxy v2 (0x47d9f61badec4378842d809077a5e87b9c996898), MCD Monitor Proxy v1 (0x93efcf86b6a7a33ae961a7ec6c741f49bce11da7), MCD Subscriptions v2 (0xc45d4f6b6bf41b6edaa58b01c4298b8d9078269a), Smart Savings (0x296420a79fe17b72eb4749ca26d4e53602f4edef), Automation Bot 3 (0xaed662abcc4fa3314985e67ea993cad064a7f5cf), Exchange (0x64c5cc449bd253d7fd57751c9080accd0216126d).

Audit schedule: Dedaub Automation Mar 2021, ConsenSys Recipe Mar 2021, Dedaub Strategies Jan 2022, Dedaub Safe Upgrade Feb 2024, Optimum BytesTransientStorage Apr 2024, Optimum TxSaver Jun 2024. Bug bounty link: https://immunefi.com/bounty/defisaver/

ConsenSys Diligence audit report for DeFi Saver conducted Mar 22 - Apr 2, 2021 (20 person-days). Commit hash cb29669a84c2d6fffaf2231c0938eb407c060919 audited. Scope: Recipe contracts, DFS Registry, RecipeExecutor, and action contracts.

Dedaub audit of Automation (Jan 2021) found no critical issues. Admin-level control noted: protocol can change gas parameters and upgrade Monitor contract behind 24h timelock. Monitor upgrade pending visible on-chain.
Knowledge base homepage links to GitHub repository (https://github.com/defisaver/defisaver-v3-contracts) and docs site.

unknowns: D5: Optimism, Base, Linea, Plasma address enumeration incomplete due to token budget; next run should fetch these JSON files to enumerate full cross-chain address set.
D6: Factory pattern not examined; DFSRegistry and other core contracts may register dynamic child addresses. Strategy/pool enumeration deferred to downstream slices.
D1: No block-explorer searches executed for Arbitrum, Optimism, Base, or other chains. Only Ethereum search completed via site: operator. Per-chain searches recommended in next run.

protocol_metadata.about: DeFi Saver is a non-custodial DeFi management dashboard enabling users to automate collateralized debt position management across multiple lending protocols (Aave, Compound, MakerDAO, Liquity, Spark, Morpho). Core features include Boost/Repay leverage automation, Loan Shifter for position migration, and Recipe Creator for composable transaction execution. Smart contracts are upgradeable via multisig with timelock (7 days for core contracts, 1 day for action contracts).
